_meta.description: Sourced values for the 8 Power Index dimensions that are NOT in the commitment ledger. Each dimension uses ONE canonical public dataset. Generated from a per-dimension research sweep 2026-06-18. Confidence and notes are honest: 'high' = read directly from the source; 'med'/'low' = proxy, derived, or judgment. Cells absent from a dimension's `values` map are genuinely unsourceable (no fabrication) and must render as n/a, never as a number.

quality_grades.ai_reg: SOLID — single authoritative index, native 0-100, 25/26 direct
quality_grades.ai_labs: SOLID for leaders; tail are genuine zeros (below frontier threshold)
quality_grades.ai_talent: SOLID for 17; CHINA MISSING by source methodology; 9 unsourced
quality_grades.q_patents: SOLID for 11 majors + EU; 14 jurisdictions genuinely unsourceable
quality_grades.ai_compute: PARTIAL — HPC proxy, NOT AI-specific; China distorted; 5 no-data
quality_grades.q_qubits: PARTIAL — data exists but cross-modality non-comparable; raw count misleading
quality_grades.q_strategy: CONSTRUCTED — transparent rubric over citable facts (strategy year, budget, institute)
quality_grades.q_export: ANALYST CONSTRUCT — no published index; best framework rescinded May-2025; only CHN defensible

ai_reg.label: AI governance maturity
ai_reg.source: Oxford Insights — Government AI Readiness Index 2024 (Government pillar)
ai_reg.method: Native 0-100 Government pillar, used directly. EU = mean of DEU/FRA/ITA/ESP/NLD (proxy).

CHN.note: Oxford flags limited transparency; likely understated

EU.note: derived proxy (member mean); EU AI Act arguably warrants higher

ai_labs.label: Frontier AI models
ai_labs.source: Epoch AI / Our World in Data — cumulative large-scale AI systems (>1e23 FLOP) by lead-developer country, 2020-2025
ai_labs.method: Count of frontier-scale models by country of lead developer. Zeros = genuine absence above threshold, not 'no AI sector'. EU = sum of member rows (overlaps members).

EU.note: sum of member rows; overlaps members

GBR.note: understated — DeepMind attributed to US parent

IND.note: active ecosystem, no >1e23 FLOP model yet

ai_talent.label: Top AI researchers
ai_talent.source: Stanford HAI AI Index 2026 / Zeki 2025 — top AI authors & inventors by work country (Fig 1.8.1)
ai_talent.method: Absolute count (thousands) by country of work, read from chart. CHINA EXCLUDED by Zeki methodology — left n/a, NOT imputed.
ai_talent.missing_note: CHN is a major hole (top-2 AI-talent power, excluded by Zeki). Needs a separately-sourced estimate or stays n/a.

q_patents.label: Quantum patent families
q_patents.source: EPO/OECD — Mapping the Global Quantum Ecosystem (Dec 2025), all patent families by applicant country, 2005-2024
q_patents.method: All patent families (thousands). NB: by international-family (IPF) metric, CHN drops to ~0.95 and USA leads — metric choice reshapes ranking. EU overlaps members.
q_patents.missing_note: Long tail dumped into 'Rest of World' in every authoritative report; genuinely unsourceable, not fabricated.

ai_compute.label: Compute capacity
ai_compute.source: TOP500 Nov-2025 Rmax (HPC proxy); Epoch AI GPU-cluster share for AI-specific cross-check
ai_compute.method: HPC FP64 proxy normalized US=100 — NOT AI-specific. Read as rank-order. China/Korea/France distorted vs AI reality (see notes).

FRA.note: proxy understates — Epoch ranks FRA #5 in AI clusters

KOR.note: proxy SEVERELY understates — Epoch ranks KOR #4 in AI clusters

CHN.note: DISPUTED — TOP500 undercount (China stopped submitting); Epoch AI-specific ~19. Do not trust 1.4.

ai_compute.missing_note: No public per-country AI-compute breakdown; Singapore especially is a data gap, not zero.

q_qubits.label: Largest qubit program
q_qubits.source: Per-vendor primary announcements 2024-2026 (Atom Computing, IBM, USTC, Fujitsu/RIKEN, Pasqal, IQM, D-Wave, Quantinuum, etc.)
q_qubits.method: Largest physical-qubit gate-model processor by country of DEVELOPER. WARNING: cross-modality non-comparable (annealing/superconducting/trapped-ion/neutral-atom not equivalent). Raw count misleads — consider ordinal tiers.

USA.note: Atom Computing neutral-atom; IBM Condor 1121 superconducting; Caltech 6100 array not yet a computer

CHN.note: Xiaohong test platform; Zuchongzhi 3.0=105 is the validated advantage processor

CAN.note: D-Wave ANNEALER — not gate-model, not comparable; gate-model CAN peak far lower

ITA.note: Pasqal(FRA)-built, hosted in IT — not indigenous

NLD.note: leads on spin-qubit quality not count

BRA.note: imported Chinese hardware, not yet operational

q_strategy.label: Quantum strategy maturity
q_strategy.source: Qureca Quantum Initiatives Worldwide 2026 + OECD national-strategies (Dec 2025) — constructed rubric
q_strategy.method: Transparent rubric over CITABLE facts: strategy existence+age (40) + committed budget tier (25) + national institute (20) + coordination body (15). The facts are hard; the 0-100 mapping is constructed.

q_export.label: Export-control exposure
q_export.source: ANALYST CONSTRUCT — no published index. Buckets from CSIS/BIS (US diffusion-rule tiers, RESCINDED May-2025; Country Group D:5; Wassenaar).
q_export.method: Bucket is citable; the 0-100 number is an analyst mapping. Only CHN (~95, Group D:5 in force) is genuinely data-backed. Within-tier ordering is judgment. RECOMMEND demoting to a 3-band categorical flag, not a continuous score.
q_export.warning: Breaks the 'every number backed by data' promise except for CHN. Demote or fence off.
